For example, if a business has already reached the Tier 2, the business would be charged Tier 2’s authentication-international rate:</li></span></ul><div class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 xpbi8i2 x9820fh xml9wff xhj0du5 xrm2kyc xjprkx4 xlu1awn x12429cg x6tc29j xbq7h4v x6jdkww xq9mrsl x14vqqas xod5an3\">Example 3: A business has 3 WABAs sending authentication messages to India. For WABA A, it is still July 31 based on their timezone. For WABAs B and C, it is already August 1 based on their timezone. For July, the business is already being charged Tier Rate 1.</div><ul class=\"x1mfogq2 xrgpmi2 xtaz4m5\"><span class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 xpbi8i2 x9820fh xml9wff xhj0du5 xrm2kyc xjprkx4 xlu1awn x12429cg x6tc29j xbq7h4v x6jdkww xq9mrsl\"><li class=\"x1xmf6yo x1e56ztr\">The business portfolio will be accruing toward tiers for both July (via WABA A) and August (via WABAs B, C) for a period of time.</li></span><span class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 xpbi8i2 x9820fh xml9wff xhj0du5 xrm2kyc xjprkx4 xlu1awn x12429cg x6tc29j xbq7h4v x6jdkww xq9mrsl\"><li class=\"x1xmf6yo x1e56ztr\">The business can reach the next tier for July, via WABA A. If that happens, messages for the remainder of July for WABA A will be charged Tier Rate 2.</li></span></ul><div class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 xpbi8i2 x9820fh xml9wff xhj0du5 xrm2kyc xjprkx4 xlu1awn x12429cg x6tc29j xbq7h4v x6jdkww xq9mrsl x14vqqas xod5an3\">Example 4: A business has 3 WABAs, integrated across 2 partners. Provider 1 sends the first B messages in a given month, and provider 2 starts sending messages as of when the business is in the 3rd tier. The business does not send enough messages that month to reach the next tier. What Meta charges each provider:</div><ul class=\"x1mfogq2 xrgpmi2 xtaz4m5\"><span class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 xpbi8i2 x9820fh xml9wff tells you the WhatsApp user country/region for which your new volume tier rate applies.</li></span></ul><div class=\"x1motxo8 x1dg8jwx x3a6nna xm5vtmc x1t2x7uc x1o1n6r0 x1366vqa x1c773n9 x1k03ns3 href=\"/documentation/business-messaging/whatsapp/webhooks/reference/account_update\" role=\"link\" tabindex=\"0\">account_update</a> webhooks to be triggered that describe the same tier switch event. (which is when Meta switched from conversation-based pricing to per-message pricing), a utility conversation is opened between you and a user that spans the switch to per-message pricing (the conversation was opened before the switch but won’t close until after the switch). In this case, utility templates sent to the user after the switch while the conversation is open will be free, but attributed to the open conversation. In status <a class=\"x1i10hfl xjbqb8w xjqpnuy xc5r6h4 xqeqjp1 x1phubyo x13fuv20 x18b5jzi x1q0q8m5 x1t7ytsu x972fbf x10w94by x1qhh985 x14e42zd x9f619 x1ypdohk xdl72j9 xdt5ytf x2lah0s x3ct3a4 xdj266r x14z9mp xat24cr x1lziwak x2lwn1j xeuugli xexx8yu xyri2b x18d9i69 x1c1uobl x16tdsg8 xggy1nq x1ja2u2z x1t137rt x1bvjpef xt0b8zv xmnv3 x7s97pk x9v5kkp xzv1bek xawggmj x1uhb9sk xt0psk2 x6s0dn4\" href=\"/documentation/business-messaging/whatsapp/webhooks/reference/messages/status\" role=\"link\" tabindex=\"0\">messages</a> webhooks, these messages will have a <code class=\"x1ey7xld x1mzt3pk xnxqytt\">pricing_model</code> of <code class=\"x1ey7xld x1mzt3pk xnxqytt\">CBP</code> and the utility conversation ID will be assigned to <code class=\"x1ey7xld x1mzt3pk xnxqytt\">conversation.id</code>.
